mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer spending strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which boosts short-term equity valuations during late-cycle expansions when supply constraints support pricing power.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the mechanism reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 82 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year CPI increase above 2.5 percent forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of high-inflation trigger months, or the negative correlation breaks down when inflation is supply-driven rather than demand-driven.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment signals structural labor market deterioration, which erodes earnings expectations and produces negative equity returns over three months.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 60-day returns in more than half of observed cases, or the joint effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising household confidence and construction sector activity, which immediately lifts short-term market sentiment and generates positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns in a majority of observations, or the signal Rank IC drops below 0.01 across rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rebounds above the 100 index baseline indicate recovering factory output, which drives positive equity returns during post-shock recovery phases as earnings expectations reset upward.
failure_condition: INDPRO crossing above 100 fails to generate positive 20-day returns in recovery regimes, or the predictive accuracy falls below 52 percent in out-of-sample validation.
